revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''築山古墳 (前原市)'''（'''つきやまこふん（まえばらし）'''）とは、[[福岡県]] [[前原市]]三雲にある[[古墳]]。[[古墳時代...'
revision.text:
'''築山古墳 (前原市)'''（'''つきやまこふん（まえばらし）'''）とは、[[福岡県]] [[前原市]]三雲にある[[古墳]]。[[古墳時代]]前期と推測される[[前方後円墳]]。 相当削られており、推定では全長60ｍあったと思われる。
北に約100ｍ離れて、[[端山古墳]]（はやまこふん：前方後円墳、推定全長80ｍ、古墳時代前期）が存在し、これらの位置は、名前が似ているだけかも知れないが、まるで、[[奈良盆地]]の[[箸墓]]と[[馬見古墳群|築山古墳]]との位置関係（約7ｋｍ離れる）を思わせる。
これらの古墳は、[[細石神社]]の北東約200ｍの所にあり、さらに、北西約1ｋｍには[[平原遺跡]]がある。 付近は[[魏志倭人伝]]の[[伊都国]]の主要な地域と考えられ、さらに、これら遺跡の関連性の調査が待たれる。築山古墳、端山古墳とも、埋葬施設の発掘は、いまだ行われていない。

'''築山古墳 (前原市)'''（'''つきやまこふん（まえばらし）'''）とは、[[福岡県]] [[前原市]]三雲にある[[古墳]]。[[古墳時代]]前期と推測される[[前方後円墳]]。 相当削られており、推定では全長60ｍあったと思われる。墳丘上に[[十一面観世音菩薩]]のお堂がある。
北に約100ｍ離れて、[[端山古墳]]（はやまこふん：前方後円墳、推定全長80ｍ、古墳時代前期）が存在し、これらの位置は、名前が似ているだけかも知れないが、まるで、[[奈良盆地]]の[[箸墓]]と[[馬見古墳群|築山古墳]]との位置関係（約7ｋｍ離れる）を思わせる。
これらの古墳は、[[細石神社]]の北東約200ｍの所にあり、さらに、北西約1ｋｍには[[平原遺跡]]がある。 付近は[[魏志倭人伝]]の[[伊都国]]の主要な地域と考えられ、さらに、これら遺跡の関連性の調査が待たれる。築山古墳、端山古墳とも、埋葬施設の発掘は、いまだ行われていない。

北に約100ｍ離れて、[[端山古墳]]（はやまこふん：前方後円墳、推定全長80ｍ、古墳時代前期）が存在し、これらの位置は、名前が似ているだけかも知れないが、まるで、[[奈良盆地]]の[[箸墓]]と[[馬見古墳群|築山古墳]]との位置関係（約7ｋｍ離れる）を思わせる。
これらの古墳は、[[細石神社]]の北東約200ｍの所にあり、さらに、北西約1ｋｍには[[平原遺跡]]がある。 付近は[[魏志倭人伝]]の[[伊都国]]の主要な地域と考えられ、さらに、これら遺跡の関連性の調査が待たれる。築山古墳、端山古墳とも、埋葬施設の発掘は、いまだ行われていない。

北に約100メートル離れて、[[端山古墳]]（はやまこふん：前方後円墳、推定全長80メートル、古墳時代前期）が存在する。<!--これらの位置は、名前が似ているだけかも知れないが、まるで、[[奈良盆地]]の[[箸墓]]と[[馬見古墳群|築山古墳]]との位置関係（約7キロメートル離れる）を思わせる。-->
これらの古墳は、[[細石神社]]の北東約200メートルの所にあり、さらに、北西約1キロメートルには[[平原遺跡]]がある。付近は[[魏志倭人伝]]の[[伊都国]]の主要な地域と考えられ、さらに、これら遺跡の関連性の調査が待たれる。築山古墳、端山古墳とも、埋葬施設の発掘調査は未実施である。

'''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲にある[[古墳]]。[[古墳時代]]前期築造と推定される[[前方後円墳]]である。墳丘はかなり削平されているが、かつては全長60メートルあったと推定されている。墳丘上には[[十一面観音]]を祀る堂が建立されている。
北に約100メートル離れて、[[端山古墳]]（はやまこふん：前方後円墳、推定全長80メートル、[[古墳時代]]前期）が存在する。<!--これらの位置は、名前が似ているだけかも知れないが、まるで、[[奈良盆地]]の[[箸墓]]と[[馬見古墳群|築山古墳]]との位置関係（約7キロメートル離れる）を思わせる。-->
これらの古墳は、[[細石神社]]の北東約200メートルの所にあり、さらに、北西約1キロメートルには[[平原遺跡]]がある。付近は[[魏志倭人伝]]の[[伊都国]]の主要な地域と考えられ、さらに、これら遺跡の関連性の調査が待たれる。築山古墳、[[端山古墳]]とも、埋葬施設の発掘調査は未実施である。

revision.comment: 「日本の古墳」フォーマットを適用しました。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳| 名称=築山古墳| 所在地=[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲字寺口| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=墳丘長60m
高さ8m| 築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃| 被葬者=不明| 史跡指定=| 出土品=土器|埴輪| 画像=[[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|築山古墳]]}} '''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲にある[[古墳]]。[[古墳時代]]中期築造と推定される帆立貝式の[[前方後円墳]]である。墳丘はかなり削平されているが、かつては全長60メートルあったと推定され、斜面には葺石が施されている。前方部の長さ約15メートル、後円部の直径は約49メートル、高さ約8メートル。盾形の周濠が廻らされており、周濠を含むと全長は約105メートルとなる。墳丘上には[[十一面観音]]を祀る堂が建立されている。 北に約100メートル離れて、[[端山古墳]]（はやまこふん：前方後円墳、推定全長80メートル、[[古墳時代]]前期）が存在する。 これらの古墳は、[[細石神社]]の北東約200メートルの所にあり、さらに、北西約1キロメートルには[[平原遺跡]]がある。付近は[[魏志倭人伝]]の[[伊都国]]の主要な地域と考えられている。 古墳に葬られた人物は、伊都国王に代わってこの地域を支配した、大和政権と深い関係を持つ豪族であろうと考えられている糸島市教育委員会。築山古墳、[[端山古墳]]とも、埋葬施設の発掘調査は未実施である。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[古墳時代]] * [[前方後円墳]] * [[端山古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 古墳マップ 築山古墳] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html 築山古墳 - 糸島、福岡市西区の情報なら伊都ロコ - 観る 遺跡・史跡] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html 築山古墳 古墳めぐりウォーキング - 古墳めぐりウォーキング in 福岡] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきやまこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:伊都国]] [[Category:糸島市]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=築山古墳| 所在地=[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲字寺口| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=墳丘長60m
高さ8m| 築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃| 被葬者=不明| 史跡指定=| 出土品=土器、埴輪| 画像=[[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|築山古墳]]}} '''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲にある[[古墳]]。[[古墳時代]]中期築造と推定される帆立貝式の[[前方後円墳]]である。墳丘はかなり削平されているが、かつては全長60メートルあったと推定され、斜面には葺石が施されている。前方部の長さ約15メートル、後円部の直径は約49メートル、高さ約8メートル。盾形の周濠が廻らされており、周濠を含むと全長は約105メートルとなる。墳丘上には[[十一面観音]]を祀る堂が建立されている。 北に約100メートル離れて、[[端山古墳]]（はやまこふん：前方後円墳、推定全長80メートル、[[古墳時代]]前期）が存在する。 これらの古墳は、[[細石神社]]の北東約200メートルの所にあり、さらに、北西約1キロメートルには[[平原遺跡]]がある。付近は[[魏志倭人伝]]の[[伊都国]]の主要な地域と考えられている。 古墳に葬られた人物は、伊都国王に代わってこの地域を支配した、大和政権と深い関係を持つ豪族であろうと考えられている糸島市教育委員会。築山古墳、[[端山古墳]]とも、埋葬施設の発掘調査は未実施である。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[古墳時代]] * [[前方後円墳]] * [[端山古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 古墳マップ 築山古墳] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html 築山古墳 - 糸島、福岡市西区の情報なら伊都ロコ - 観る 遺跡・史跡] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html 築山古墳 古墳めぐりウォーキング - 古墳めぐりウォーキング in 福岡] {{DEFAULTSORT:つきやまこふん}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:伊都国]] [[Category:糸島市]]

{{日本の古墳| 名称=築山古墳| 所在地=[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲字寺口| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|}}| 形状=前方後円墳| 規模=墳丘長60m
高さ8m| 築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃| 被葬者=不明| 史跡指定=| 出土品=土器、埴輪| 画像=[[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|築山古墳]]}} '''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲にある[[古墳]]。[[古墳時代]]中期築造と推定される帆立貝式の[[前方後円墳]]である。墳丘はかなり削平されているが、かつては全長60メートルあったと推定され、斜面には葺石が施されている。前方部の長さ約15メートル、後円部の直径は約49メートル、高さ約8メートル。盾形の周濠が廻らされており、周濠を含むと全長は約105メートルとなる。墳丘上には[[十一面観音]]を祀る堂が建立されている。 北に約100メートル離れて、[[端山古墳]]（はやまこふん：前方後円墳、推定全長80メートル、[[古墳時代]]前期）が存在する。 これらの古墳は、[[細石神社]]の北東約200メートルの所にあり、さらに、北西約1キロメートルには[[平原遺跡]]がある。付近は[[魏志倭人伝]]の[[伊都国]]の主要な地域と考えられている。 古墳に葬られた人物は、伊都国王に代わってこの地域を支配した、大和政権と深い関係を持つ豪族であろうと考えられている糸島市教育委員会。築山古墳、[[端山古墳]]とも、埋葬施設の発掘調査は未実施である。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[古墳時代]] * [[前方後円墳]] * [[端山古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 古墳マップ 築山古墳] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html 築山古墳 - 糸島、福岡市西区の情報なら伊都ロコ - 観る 遺跡・史跡] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html 築山古墳 古墳めぐりウォーキング - 古墳めぐりウォーキング in 福岡] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきやまこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:伊都国]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

revision.text:
[[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Tsukiyama Kofun | Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}{{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}} Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | Coordinates = {{coord|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Mound length 60 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}}
Height 8m | Construction Period = Around the end of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | Person Buried = Unknown | Historic Site Designation = | Excavated Items = {{ill|Pottery|ja|土器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | image = [[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|Tsukiyama Kofun]] }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲}}, {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}} with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Saiseki Shrine|ja|細石神社}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Site|ja|平原遺跡}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|ja|伊都国}}, as described in the {{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun Map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Information on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Itokoku]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Kk]] [[en:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.text:
[[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] {{Infobox Kofun in Japan | Name = Tsukiyama Kofun | Location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}{{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}} Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | Coordinates = {{coord|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline}} | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} | Size = Mound length 60 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}}
Height 8m | Construction Period = Around the end of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | Person Buried = Unknown | Historic Site Designation = | Excavated Items = {{ill|Pottery|ja|土器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | image = [[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|Tsukiyama Kofun]] }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲}}, {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}} with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Saiseki Shrine|ja|細石神社}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Site|ja|平原遺跡}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|ja|伊都国}}, as described in the {{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}} * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun Map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Information on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Itokoku]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Kk]] [[en:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}} with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. The area is considered a key region of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|ja|伊都国}}, as described in the {{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the {{ill|Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Saiseki Shrine|ja|細石神社}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Site|ja|平原遺跡}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|ja|伊都国}}, as described in the {{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun Map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Information on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Itokoku]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Kk]] [[en:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]]

Height 8m | built = Around the end of the {{ill|4th century|ja|4世紀}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | other_designation = | Excavated Items = {{ill|Pottery|ja|土器}}, {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | image = [[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|Tsukiyama Kofun]] }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲}}, {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}. It is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 {{ill|meters|ja|メートル}} with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|ja|十一面観音}}. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Saiseki Shrine|ja|細石神社}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Site|ja|平原遺跡}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|ja|伊都国|1=Itokoku}}, as described in the {{ill|Records of Wei|ja|魏志倭人伝}}. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and other_designations] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Itokoku]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:11]] [[en:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]]

15 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:築山古墳 (糸島市) up to rev 68361154
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=築山古墳 |所在地=[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲字寺口 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長60[[メートル|m]]
高さ8m |築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃 |被葬者=不明 |史跡指定= |出土品=[[土器]]、[[埴輪]] |画像=[[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|築山古墳]]}} '''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲にある[[古墳]]。[[古墳時代]]中期築造と推定される帆立貝式の[[前方後円墳]]である。墳丘はかなり削平されているが、かつては全長60[[メートル]]あったと推定され、斜面には[[葺石]]が施されている。前方部の長さ約15メートル、後円部の直径は約49メートル、高さ約8メートル。[[盾]]形の周濠が廻らされており、周濠を含むと全長は約105メートルとなる。墳丘上には[[十一面観音]]を祀る堂が建立されている。 北に約100メートル離れて、[[端山古墳]]（はやまこふん：前方後円墳、推定全長80メートル、[[古墳時代]]前期）が存在する。 これらの古墳は、[[細石神社]]の北東約200メートルの所にあり、さらに、北西約1[[キロメートル]]には[[平原遺跡]]がある。付近は[[魏志倭人伝]]の[[伊都国]]の主要な地域と考えられている。 古墳に葬られた人物は、伊都国王に代わってこの地域を支配した、大和政権と深い関係を持つ豪族であろうと考えられている糸島市教育委員会。築山古墳、[[端山古墳]]とも、埋葬施設の[[発掘調査]]は未実施である。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[古墳時代]] * [[前方後円墳]] * [[端山古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 古墳マップ 築山古墳] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html 築山古墳 - 糸島、福岡市西区の情報なら伊都ロコ - 観る 遺跡・史跡] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html 築山古墳 古墳めぐりウォーキング - 古墳めぐりウォーキング in 福岡] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきやまこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:伊都国]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=築山古墳 |所在地=[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲字寺口 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=墳丘長60[[メートル|m]]
高さ8m |築造年代=[[4世紀]]末頃 |被葬者=不明 |史跡指定= |出土品=[[土器]]、[[埴輪]] |画像=[[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|築山古墳]]}} '''築山古墳'''（つきやまこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]三雲にある[[古墳]]。[[古墳時代]]中期築造と推定される帆立貝式の[[前方後円墳]]である。墳丘はかなり削平されているが、かつては全長60[[メートル]]あったと推定され、斜面には[[葺石]]が施されている。前方部の長さ約15メートル、後円部の直径は約49メートル、高さ約8メートル。[[盾]]形の周濠が廻らされており、周濠を含むと全長は約105メートルとなる。墳丘上には[[十一面観音]]を祀る堂が建立されている。 北に約100メートル離れて、[[端山古墳]]（はやまこふん：前方後円墳、推定全長80メートル、[[古墳時代]]前期）が存在する。 これらの古墳は、[[細石神社]]の北東約200メートルの所にあり、さらに、北西約1[[キロメートル]]には[[平原遺跡]]がある。付近は[[魏志倭人伝]]の[[伊都国]]の主要な地域と考えられている。 古墳に葬られた人物は、伊都国王に代わってこの地域を支配した、大和政権と深い関係を持つ豪族であろうと考えられている糸島市教育委員会。築山古墳、[[端山古墳]]とも、埋葬施設の[[発掘調査]]は未実施である。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[日本の古墳一覧]] * [[古墳時代]] * [[前方後円墳]] * [[端山古墳]] == 外部リンク == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 古墳マップ 築山古墳] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html 築山古墳 - 糸島、福岡市西区の情報なら伊都ロコ - 観る 遺跡・史跡] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html 築山古墳 古墳めぐりウォーキング - 古墳めぐりウォーキング in 福岡] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つきやまこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:伊都国]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]] Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]]
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = [[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|Tsukiyama Kofun]] }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|qq=}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|qq=}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Itokoku]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[en:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳]]

Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Itokoku]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[en:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:伊都国]] [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマト王権}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Itokoku]] [[Category:History of Itoshima City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:伊都国]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category History of Itoshima City
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]] Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]]
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = [[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|Tsukiyama Kofun]] }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|qq=}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマト王権}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Itokoku]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:伊都国]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマト王権}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:伊都国]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Qq
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]] Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]]
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = [[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|Tsukiyama Kofun]] }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|qq=}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマト王権}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:伊都国]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]]

The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマト王権}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]] Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{coord|33|32|19.62|N|130|14|41.34|E|region:JP-40_type:landmark|display=inline}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]]
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = [[File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg|280px|Tsukiyama Kofun]] }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|qq=}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|qq=}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]]

The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|qq=}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[en:{{subst:FULLPAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11603504]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]] Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{Coord|33.538783333333|N|130.24481666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]]
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}} }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|qq=}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|qq=}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]]

Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]]}} [[sn:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tsukiyama Kofun (Itoshima)]][[Category:qq]]

The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]] Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{Coord|33.538783333333|N|130.24481666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]]
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|qq=|1=Mikumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132132121}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|qq=|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}}

The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}}

revision.text:
[[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11603504]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]] Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{Coord|33.538783333333|N|130.24481666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]]
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|1=Mikumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132132121}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
