Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|1=Mikumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132132121}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}}

revision.comment: Consolidate multiple wikidata link templates into single one at end
revision.text:
[[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11603504]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]] Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{Coord|33.538783333333|N|130.24481666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]]
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|1=Mikumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132132121}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}}

Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}}

Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|1=Mikumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132132121}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11603504]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]][[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]] Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{Coord|33.538783333333|N|130.24481666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Mound length 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]]
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the [[:en:4th century|4th century]] | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = [[:en:Earthenware|Pottery]], [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] | image = File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|1=Mikumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132132121}}, [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 [[:en:Metre|meters]] with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the [[:en:Ekādaśamukha|Eleven-faced Kannon]]. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient [[:en:Itokoku|Itokoku]], as described in the [[:en:Wajinden|Records of Wei]]. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon}}. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|WD=Q10885162|lt=Itokoku}}, as described in the {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Records of Wei}}. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|WD=Q10885162|lt=Itokoku}}, as described in the {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Records of Wei}}. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11603504]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima}} Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{Coord|33.538783333333|N|130.24481666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Mound length 60 {{ill|Metre|en|Metre|WD=Q11573|lt=meters}}
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = {{ill|Earthenware|en|Earthenware|WD=Q1377111|lt=Pottery}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} | image = File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|1=Mikumo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132132121}}, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 {{ill|Metre|en|Metre|WD=Q11573|lt=meters}} with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon}}. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|WD=Q10885162|lt=Itokoku}}, as described in the {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Records of Wei}}. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kinship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Hayama Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}}. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Q18338882|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|WD=Q10885162|lt=Itokoku|1=Itokoku}}, as described in the {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Records of Wei|1=Wajinden}}. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Q18338882|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tsukiyama Kofun | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima|1=Itoshima}} Mikumo Aza Teraguchi | coordinates = {{Coord|33.538783333333|N|130.24481666667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} | area = Mound length 60 {{ill|Metre|en|Metre|WD=Q11573|lt=meters|1=Metre}}
Height 8m | built = Around the end of the {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|WD=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} | Interred_individual = Unknown | Historic Site = | burial goods = {{ill|Earthenware|en|Earthenware|WD=Q1377111|lt=Pottery|1=Earthenware}}, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | image = File:Tsukiyama Kohun.jpg | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }} '''Tsukiyama Kofun''' (築山古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Mikumo|ja|三雲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumo|1=Q132132121|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q132132121}}, {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima|1=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}, estimated to have been constructed in the middle of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. Although the mound has been significantly flattened, it is believed to have originally had a total length of 60 {{ill|Metre|en|Metre|WD=Q11573|lt=meters|1=Metre}} with stone coverings (*fukiishi*) applied to its slopes. The front section is approximately 15 meters long, while the rear circular section has a diameter of about 49 meters and a height of roughly 8 meters. A {{ill|shield-shaped moat|ja|盾形の周濠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shield-shaped moat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} surrounds the mound, and when including the moat, the total length reaches around 105 meters. Atop the mound, there is a hall enshrining the {{ill|Ekādaśamukha|en|Ekādaśamukha|WD=Q11404731|lt=Eleven-faced Kannon|1=Ekādaśamukha}}. Approximately 100 meters to the north lies the {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Q18338882|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} (前方後円墳), estimated to have a total length of 80 meters, constructed in the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. These tumuli are located about 200 meters northeast of {{ill|Sazareishi Shrine|ja|細石神社|1=Sazareishi Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sazareishi Shrine|qq=}}, with the {{ill|Hirabaru Ruins|ja|平原遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirabaru Site|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} approximately 1 kilometer to the northwest. The area is considered a key region of the ancient {{ill|Itokoku|en|Itokoku|WD=Q10885162|lt=Itokoku|1=Itokoku}}, as described in the {{ill|Wajinden|en|Wajinden|WD=Q1135798|lt=Records of Wei|1=Wajinden}}. The individuals buried in the tumulus are thought to have been powerful local chieftains closely connected to the {{ill|Yamato polity|ja|大和政権|1=Yamato Kingship|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato polity|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2658252}}, ruling this area in place of the Itokoku kings.Itoshima City Board of Education Neither the Tsukiyama Kofun nor the Hayama Kofun has undergone archaeological excavation of their burial facilities. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} * {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}} * {{ill|Hayama Kofun|ja|端山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayama Kofun|1=Q18338882|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q18338882}} == External Links == * [http://kofun.info/kofun/932 Kofun map - Tsukiyama Kofun] * [http://www.itoloco.com/detail/index_422.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Intypeation on Itoshima and Nishi-ku, Fukuoka from Ito Loco - Ruins and Historic Sites] * [http://kofunmeguriwalking.web.fc2.com/tsukiyama.html Tsukiyama Kofun - Kofun Meguri Walking in Fukuoka] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{translated page|ja|築山古墳 (糸島市)|version=68361154|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:築山古墳 (糸島市)]] [[ja:築山古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11603504}} [[Category:Ito Province]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōen-fun]]
